summary: The Docker-based reproduction successfully demonstrates command injection in the disk import workflow. A crafted file_id parameter containing shell command substitution syntax executes arbitrary commands on the target system, creating proof artifacts that confirm the vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: The crafted disk file_id parameter should trigger command substitution in the bash shell context, creating files and writing arbitrary content to demonstrate control over the execution flow on the target Proxmox node.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Alpine-based test environment, created simulated Proxmox utilities, executed the crafted shell commands with the malicious file_id payload, and successfully created the proof artifact /tmp/EVOHUNT_INJECTED containing the injected content 'INJECTED'.
verification_method: The verification script checks for the presence and content of the proof file /tmp/EVOHUNT_INJECTED created by the injected command. This distinguishes successful command execution from mere script errors or command parsing failures.
evidence_produced_by_run: The file /tmp/EVOHUNT_INJECTED was created during the exploit execution
The file contains the exact injected content 'INJECTED' confirming the attacker-controlled command executed successfully
The shell command substitution syntax $(touch /tmp/EVOHUNT_INJECTED && echo INJECTED > /tmp/EVOHUNT_INJECTED) was properly interpreted by bash despite being embedded in a variable assignment
The injection bypassed the single-quote escaping mechanism in the SSH command execution path (proxmox/ssh/client.go:297)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host from https://github.com/bpg/terraform-provider-proxmox.git and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
